explanation.html_text:
Question / ప్రశ్న

Teaching is said to be very effectively planned when

బోధన ప్రభావంతంగా ప్రణాళిక చేయబడిందని ఎప్పుడు అనవచ్చు

  1. Needs of at least fifty percent of students are targetedకనీసం యాభై శాతం విద్యార్థుల అవసరాలు లక్ష్యంగా పెట్టుకున్నప్పుడు
  2. Usage of only textbook is practicedపాఠ్యపుస్తకాన్ని మాత్రమే ఉపయోగించుటను ఆచరించినప్పుడు
  3. Framing of the objectives after the completion of lessonపాఠం పూర్తయిన తర్వాత లక్ష్యాలను రూపొందించినప్పుడు
  4. All the children in the class are engaged in the process of learningతరగతిలోని విద్యార్థులందరూ బోధనా ప్రక్రియలో నిమగ్నమై ఉన్నప్పుడు

Correct answer: D

All the children in the class are engaged in the process of learning

తరగతిలోని విద్యార్థులందరూ బోధనా ప్రక్రియలో నిమగ్నమై ఉన్నప్పుడు

Why this is right

Effective teaching (ప్రభావవంతమైన బోధన) is measured by student engagement (విద్యార్థుల నిమగ్నత), not by coverage of content or teacher activity alone. When ALL children in the class are actively engaged in the learning process, it indicates the teacher has successfully planned lessons that are inclusive, differentiated, and meaningful for every learner. This is the gold standard of lesson planning in NCF and NCFTE frameworks. The word "all" (అందరూ) is the key — it signals universal inclusion, not partial reach.

Why the others are wrong

A — 50% of students targeted: Aiming for only half the class is not effective planning; it excludes half the learners. Inclusive education demands 100% participation.
A — 50% విద్యార్థులను లక్ష్యంగా చేసుకోవడం: సగం తరగతిని లక్ష్యంగా చేసుకోవడం సమగ్ర ప్రణాళిక కాదు.

B — Only textbook used: Sole reliance on the textbook limits engagement and ignores learner diversity. Good planning uses multiple resources.
B — కేవలం పాఠ్యపుస్తకం మాత్రమే: విద్యార్థుల వైవిధ్యాన్ని విస్మరించే పద్ధతి ప్రభావవంతమైనది కాదు.

C — Objectives framed after the lesson: Objectives must be set BEFORE instruction begins. Framing them after the lesson is completed reverses the proper planning sequence (Backward design is different — it plans assessment before instruction, but still before delivery, not after).
C — పాఠం తర్వాత లక్ష్యాలు రూపొందించడం: లక్ష్యాలు బోధనకు ముందే నిర్ణయించాలి, తర్వాత కాదు.

Concept notes

Effective Teaching Planning / ప్రభావవంతమైన బోధన ప్రణాళిక: A well-planned lesson ensures that every child — regardless of ability, background, or learning style — is actively involved. This principle aligns with the Right to Education Act (RTE 2009), NCF 2005, and NCFTE 2009, all of which emphasise inclusive, child-centred education (శిశు-కేంద్రిత విద్య).

Lesson Planning sequence (పాఠ ప్రణాళిక క్రమం): (1) Set objectives → (2) Plan activities → (3) Choose resources → (4) Teach → (5) Assess. Objectives must always precede instruction.

Student Engagement (విద్యార్థుల నిమగ్నత): Research consistently shows that active engagement — not passive listening — leads to deeper learning (Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development, Dewey's learning by doing). A teacher who plans for engagement plans for ALL learners, not a majority.

Inclusion (సమ్మిళిత విద్య): Planning that targets only 50% fails the principle of Universal Design for Learning (UDL). Effective planning = differentiated instruction for all.

Likely spin-off TET questions

  1. Which of the following is the FIRST step in lesson planning? (a) Writing objectives (b) Selecting teaching aids (c) Evaluating students (d) Framing activities) — Answer: Writing objectives / లక్ష్యాలు రాయడం
  2. According to NCF 2005, effective teaching is characterised by — (a) Completing the syllabus on time (b) Using only the textbook (c) Active participation of all learners (d) Targeting above-average students) — Answer: Active participation of all learners
  3. A teacher is said to be an effective planner when her lesson caters to — (a) Gifted students only (b) The majority of students (c) Every child in the classroom (d) Students who ask questions) — Answer: Every child in the classroom / తరగతిలోని ప్రతి విద్యార్థికి

Memory hook

"ALL in = Effective Plan; SOME in = Partial Plan."
అందరూ నిమగ్నమైతే — అద్భుతమైన ప్రణాళిక (Andaru nimagnамайтే = Adbhutamaina praṇāḷika). Remember: the NTA answer always favours 100% inclusion over any partial measure.
